metaTitle: Property Partition Suit Procedure Bangladesh 2026 Law Guide
title: Family Property Partition Suit in Bangladesh: How to Divide Joint Land & Inheritance
metaDescription: Step-by-step legal guide to filing a Partition Suit (বাটোয়ারা মামলা) in Bangladesh — preliminary decree, Saham partition, surveyor commissioner, and stopping illegal land sale.
keywords: family property division legal process Dhaka
heroIntro: Joint family property disputes and inheritance distribution conflicts are among the most common civil court litigations in Bangladesh. When siblings, relatives, or co-sharers (সহ-শরীক) refuse to divide inherited land amicably or attempt to sell specific prime portions without mutual consent, filing a Partition Suit (বাটোয়ারা মামলা) under the Partition Act 1893 and Code of Civil Procedure 1908 is the ultimate legal remedy. For expert civil representation, consult Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam.

quickAnswer.heading: ⚡ Quick Summary: Partition Suit in Bangladesh
points: Legal Forum: Filed in the Senior Assistant Judge or Sub-Judge Court having territorial jurisdiction.
Pre-requisite: Must have joint title/possession as a legitimate co-sharer (সহ-শরীক).
Two-Stage Decree: Preliminary Decree (declares share ratio) & Final Decree (demarcates physical boundary).
Court Commissioner: Advocate Commissioner/Surveyor appointed to inspect and divide land physically (Saham).
Injunction Relief: Court can issue temporary injunction stopping co-sharers from selling or building on joint land.

sections.h2: What Is a Partition Suit (বাটোয়ারা মামলা) under Bangladesh Civil Law?
sections.content:
A Partition Suit is a civil lawsuit filed by a co-owner or legal heir seeking judicial division and separate physical possession of their rightful share in joint land, building, or ancestral estate.

Governing Laws:

  • The Partition Act 1893: Provides for judicial sale or buyout of property if physical partition is impossible.
  • Code of Civil Procedure 1908 (CPC): Governs suit procedure, injunctions (Order 39), and commissioner appointment (Order 26 Rule 13).
  • Personal Inheritance Laws: Muslim Muslim Personal Law (Shariat) Application Act 1937 or Hindu Law governing share ratios.

Consult a skilled Land & Property Lawyer in Dhaka before filing.

sections.h2: When Should You File a Partition Suit?
sections.content:
You should immediately file a partition suit if:

  • Co-sharers refuse to execute a registered Deed of Amicable Partition (আপস বাটোয়ারা দলিল).
  • One co-sharer forcibly occupies the best commercial roadside portion of joint land.
  • A co-sharer secretly attempts to sell specific land plots to a third-party developer or buyer.
  • There is a dispute over exact inheritance share ratios under CS, SA, RS, or BS Khatians.

Before filing the suit, compile complete title flow records:

  • Ancestral Khatian records (CS, SA, RS, BS Khatian copies).
  • Original Death Certificate of ancestor and official Warishan Certificate.
  • Via Deeds (বায়া দলিল) showing how ancestors acquired the property.
  • Latest E-Namjari and Land Development Tax (Khajna) receipts.
  • Site map/sketch showing current joint possession.

sections.h2: Understanding the Two Stages of Partition Suit: Preliminary & Final Decree
sections.content:
A partition suit is unique because it proceeds in two distinct judicial stages:

  1. Stage 1: Preliminary Decree (প্রাথমিক ডিক্রি): The Court evaluates title documents and passes a judgment declaring the exact legal percentage/fractional share of each co-sharer (e.g., 1/4th or 2/16th share).
  2. Stage 2: Final Decree (চূড়ান্ত ডিক্রি): After the preliminary decree, the Court appoints a Commissioner to physically measure the ground area and draw up specific boundary lots (Saham). Once approved, the Court signs the Final Decree granting exclusive possession.

sections.h2: Role of Advocate Commissioner & Saham (সহম) Allocation
sections.content:
During the Final Decree stage, an official Advocate Commissioner (অ্যাডভোকেট কমিশনার) along with a professional government surveyor (আমিন) visits the land:

  • Measures exact plot dimensions, road access, and building structures.
  • Prepares individual Saham (সহম) maps demarcating exact plots for each co-sharer.
  • Submits a Commissioner Report to the Court. Parties can file written objections if dissatisfied with the proposed division.

sections.h2: Obtaining an Injunction to Stop Co-Sharers from Selling Joint Land
sections.content:
Under Order 39 Rules 1 & 2 of CPC, your advocate can file an urgent application for Temporary Injunction (অস্থায়ী নিষেধাজ্ঞা) at the time of filing the suit.

If granted:

  • Co-sharers are legally prohibited from transferring, selling, or mortgaging joint land.
  • All ongoing illegal construction work on the disputed plot is frozen by court order.
  • Sub-Registry offices are notified not to register any third-party deeds for the property.

sections.h2: Court Fees & Valuation Rules for Partition Lawsuits
sections.content:
Court fee for a partition suit depends on possession status:

  • If Plaintiff is in Joint Possession: Fixed nominal court fee (BDT 300 to BDT 1,000) applies under Court Fees Act 1870.
  • If Plaintiff has been completely dispossessed (Out of Possession): Ad Valorem court fee applies based on the market value of the plaintiff's share.

sections.h2: Why You Need a Senior Civil Advocate in Dhaka
sections.content:
Partition lawsuits involve intricate boundary math, khatian vetting, and commissioner advocacy.

Advocate Md. Shah Alam — Supreme Court Advocate practicing at Civil Courts Dhaka — has successfully represented hundreds of families in resolving joint land partition suits and securing exclusive title decrees.

⚖️ Need to Divide Joint Family Property? Contact Supreme Court Advocate Advocate Md. Shah Alam for expert civil partition suit representation. Visit our Land Legal Services Page or Contact Us Directly.

faqs.question: Can a co-sharer sell their specific plot before a partition suit is finalized?
faqs.answer: A co-sharer can legally sell only their undivided fractional share, but CANNOT transfer a specific physical plot or portion without an executed partition deed or court final decree. Any such sale is subject to court injunction.

faqs.question: How long does a partition suit take in Bangladesh courts?
faqs.answer: An uncontested partition suit takes approx 1 to 2 years. A contested suit involving multiple appeals can take 3 to 5 years to reach Final Decree.

faqs.question: What if physical partition of land or building is impossible?
faqs.answer: Under the Partition Act 1893, if a building or narrow plot cannot be physically divided without destroying its value, the court can order an auction sale among co-sharers or to the public, distributing money according to share ratios.

faqs.question: What is an Amicable Partition Deed (আপস বাটোয়ারা দলিল)?
faqs.answer: If all co-sharers agree mutually, they can avoid court by drafting an Amicable Partition Deed, getting it registered at the Sub-Registry office, and mutating individual Khatians.
